cards.grammarNote: 越来越 + adjective/verb = more and more / increasingly. It expresses a continuing change in one direction.

cards.front: My health is getting better and better.
cards.romanized: Wǒ de shēntǐ yuè lái yuè hǎo le.

cards.front: My legs are hurting more and more.
cards.romanized: Wǒ de tuǐ yuè lái yuè téng.

cards.front: After walking so far, I'm getting more and more tired.
cards.back: 走了这么远，我越来越累。
cards.romanized: Zǒu le zhème yuǎn, wǒ yuè lái yuè lèi.

cards.front: The more I eat, the more I want to eat.
cards.grammarNote: 越...越... links two actions that escalate together. The first 越 sets the condition; the second 越 gives the consequence.

cards.front: The more I walk, the more tired I get.
The more rest you get, the better.
His illness is getting more and more serious.
cards.romanized: Tā de bìng yuè lái yuè zhòng.

cards.front: The doctor is getting more and more worried.
